mission.title: litellm: Why does OTel tracing require `litellm[proxy]`?
mission.description: Why does OTel tracing require `litellm[proxy]`?. Requested by 21+ users.

steps.title: Check current litellm deployment
steps.description:
Verify your litellm version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n litellm -l app.kubernetes.io/name=litellm
helm list -n litellm 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working litellm installation.

steps.title: Review litellm configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant litellm configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n litellm -l app.kubernetes.io/name=litellm
kubectl get configmap -n litellm -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=litellm
```
I'd like to use OpenTelemetry tracing with LiteLLM. But this causes an error:

```
ImportError: Missing dependency No module named 'backoff'.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Why does OTel tracing require `litellm[proxy]`?
steps.description:
Apply the configuration change to resolve the issue:
```yaml
ImportError: Missing dependency No module named 'backoff'. Run `pip install 'litellm[proxy]'`
ERROR    LiteLLM:litellm_logging.py:3416 [Non-Blocking Error] Error initializing custom logger: Missing dependency No module named 'backoff'. Run `pip install 'litellm[proxy]'`
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/.venv/lib/python3.12/site-packages/litellm/proxy/proxy_server.py", line 74, in <module>
    import backoff
ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'backoff'

During handling of the above exception, another exception occurred:

Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/.venv/lib/python3
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n litellm -l app.kubernetes.io/name=litellm
kubectl get events -n litellm --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Why does OTel tracing require `litellm[proxy]`?" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: See the source issue for the community-verified solution: https://github.com/BerriAI/litellm/issues/13081

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with litellm installed or the issue environment reproducible.
